commit.message:
v0.1.1: Wakeup broadcast on disconnect instead of process restart

The wallbox stops sending UDP broadcasts after authentication. When no
datagram is received for 35 seconds, instead of restarting the process,
the WiFiManager now:
- Resets connection and device state (initialization_state, logged_in)
- Sends a discovery broadcast (login beacon with all-FF serial/password)
  to 255.255.255.255 on the wallbox's last known source port
- Keeps the UDP socket open and reschedules the check every 35 seconds

This triggers the wallbox to resume broadcasting without requiring a
full addon restart or manual app intervention.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: web console reads ZYLOS_WEB_PASSWORD with fallback to legacy name (#248)

zylos init writes ZYLOS_WEB_PASSWORD but server.js only read
WEB_CONSOLE_PASSWORD, causing auth to silently fail on new installs.

- server.js: read ZYLOS_WEB_PASSWORD first, fall back to WEB_CONSOLE_PASSWORD
- SKILL.md: update docs to reference ZYLOS_WEB_PASSWORD
- .env.example: update template variable name

commit.message:
Add native PulseAudio driver for modern Linux systems

This commit implements native PulseAudio support to fix audio on modern
Linux distributions where the ALSA plugin method is not available.

Changes:
- Add RageSoundDriver_PulseAudio driver using libpulse-simple API
- Configure PulseAudio detection via pkg-config in configure.ac
- Register PulseAudio as the default driver for Linux (before ALSA)
- Integrate PulseAudio into build system (Makefile.am)

Technical details:
- Uses pa_simple API for straightforward integration
- 44.1kHz, 16-bit stereo output
- ~93ms latency configuration
- Thread-safe mixing via RageSound_Generic_Software base class
- Compatible with both PulseAudio and PipeWire

This resolves "No such file or directory" errors when attempting to
open ALSA devices on systems running PulseAudio/PipeWire.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: add eval_type_backport for Python 3.9 Pydantic compatibility

Pydantic evaluates type annotations at runtime, so `int | None` syntax
fails on Python 3.9. The eval_type_backport package provides runtime
support for PEP 604 union syntax on older Python versions.

commit.message:
Add end-to-end diff-file lifecycle benchmark

Exercises the real pipeline: git diff -> DiffParser ->
SyntaxHighlightService -> Livewire render. No mocking - uses a temp
git repo with realistic PHP files to force Phiki through non-trivial
tokenization. Three test cases: small (1 hunk), large (~200 LOC,
multi-hunk), and separated multi-hunk.

Provides baseline timings for future optimization work.

Prevent worker crashes from after-callback exceptions and add before_job hook

Wrap $after callbacks in try-catch in BuryJob and CompleteJob so a failing
callback no longer propagates out of the catch block and crashes the worker.
Add a configurable before_job callback that runs before each job, allowing
applications to reconnect stale database connections between jobs.

commit.message:
fix: prevent false positive duplicate detection on NF import

- Product codes in names (FCD2066 vs FCD2093) must match exactly
- Different codes = different products, regardless of name similarity
- Remove substring matching that caused false positives
- Restore threshold to 60% for safer matching

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: enable `SSL session caching` and `HTTP agent reuse` for faster consecutive requests (#6987)

* fix: enable SSL session caching for faster consecutive requests (#6929)

* fix: enable SSL session caching for faster consecutive requests

Previously, Bruno created a new HTTPS agent for every request, which meant
SSL/TLS sessions couldn't be reused. This caused the full TLS handshake
(~450ms) to run on every request, even to the same endpoint.

Changes:
- Add agent caching based on TLS configuration (certs, proxy, SSL options)
- Reuse cached agents for requests with matching configuration
- SSL sessions are now cached and reused, significantly reducing
  response time for consecutive requests to the same host

The fix maintains backward compatibility:
- Timeline logging moved to setup phase (before agent creation)
- Proxy and SSL validation behavior unchanged
- Added clearAgentCache() for testing and configuration changes

Fixes #5574

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address review feedback for SSL session caching

- Add passphrase to cache key to prevent incorrect agent reuse
- Add MAX_AGENT_CACHE_SIZE (100) with LRU-style eviction
- Use consistent node: prefix for crypto import

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(preferences): add cache.httpHttpsAgents.enabled preference

* feat(agent-cache): add disableCache option to getOrCreateAgent

* feat(proxy-util): respect httpHttpsAgents cache preference

* refactor(agent-cache): use named props for getOrCreateAgent and getOrCreateHttpAgent

* feat(ipc): add renderer:clear-http-https-agent-cache handler

* feat(redux): add cache.httpHttpsAgents preferences to initial state

* feat(ui): add Cache tab to Preferences

* feat(cli): add --disable-http-https-agents-cache flag

* refactor(cache): replace window.ipcRenderer calls with redux actions

Add getCacheStats, purgeCache, and clearHttpHttpsAgentCache thunks to
the app slice. Update the Cache preferences component to dispatch these
actions instead of calling window.ipcRenderer directly.

Also move handleSave and handleSaveRef above useFormik to fix declaration
order — onSubmit closes over handleSaveRef, so the ref must be initialized
before useFormik is called.

* fix: tests

* fix(cache): thread disableCache and hostname through all agent-creation paths

- Forward disableHttpHttpsAgentsCache through getHttpHttpsAgents → createAgents
  so OAuth2 token requests and bru.sendRequest honour the CLI flag
- Add hostname to agent cache keys (getAgentCacheKey, getHttpAgentCacheKey)
  for per-host TLS session reuse; extract hostname at every call site in
  run-single-request.js, proxy-util.js, and http-https-agents.ts
- Add extractHostname helper in http-https-agents.ts to safely parse hostnames
- Add test coverage for cert, key, pfx, passphrase, and hostname cache-key
  differentiation in agent-cache.spec.ts

* refactor(cache): rename getOrCreateAgent to getOrCreateHttpsAgent

* refactor: simplify UI labels, optimize agent timeline wrapping, silence proxy errors

* fix: tests

* fix(proxy): fix proxy agent construction and CA cert handling

Three fixes:

1. Proxy agents (HttpsProxyAgent, HttpProxyAgent, SocksProxyAgent) expect
   (proxyUri, options) constructor signature, but the agent cache was packing
   proxyUri into options as a single argument. Fixed the non-timeline code
   path in getOrCreateAgentInternal.

2. HTTP requests through an HTTPS proxy need TLS options (ca certs) to
   validate the proxy's certificate. All getOrCreateHttpAgent call sites
   now pass TLS options when the proxy protocol is HTTPS.

3. Setting the `ca` option on any Node.js TLS connection replaces the
   default OpenSSL trust store entirely. CAs only in the OpenSSL default
   trust store (e.g. /etc/ssl/cert.pem) but not in tls.rootCertificates
   were lost. Fixed by converting `ca` to a secureContext via addCACert(),
   which appends custom CAs on top of the OpenSSL defaults instead of
   replacing them.

Also simplified PatchedHttpsProxyAgent to selectively forward only the
relevant TLS options (cert, key, pfx, passphrase, rejectUnauthorized,
secureContext) to the target TLS upgrade instead of blindly merging all
constructor options.

* fix(tls): load client certs into secureContext to prevent silent drop

Add Cache tab to Preferences UI

* fix(proxy): align proxy auth check to use auth.disabled field consistently

* refactor(cache): rename CLI flag to --cache-ssl-session and disable caching by default

- Rename --disable-http-https-agents-cache to --cache-ssl-session (opt-in)
- Rename disableHttpHttpsAgentsCache to cacheSslSession across CLI and bruno-requests
- Default caching to disabled in both bruno-electron and bruno-cli
- Add cacheSslSession to buildCertsAndProxyConfig for bru.sendRequest
- Update Preferences UI labels to "Cache SSL Session"

* refactor(cache): rename httpHttpsAgents to sslSession across preferences and UI

* refactor(cache): remove unused getCacheStats and purgeCache IPC actions

commit.message:
Further stylus optimizations: stable listeners, cached rect, desynchronized canvas

Three additional fixes for stylus responsiveness:

1. Stable event listeners: useEffect now runs once (empty deps) with
   refs for mutable values, eliminating the detach/reattach gap that
   could cause missed pointerdown events during React re-renders.

2. Cached getBoundingClientRect: avoids forced synchronous layout
   reflow on every pointer event. Cache invalidated on scroll/resize.

3. desynchronized canvas context: bypasses browser compositing pipeline
   for lower-latency ink rendering, especially beneficial for stylus.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add step-by-step debug info to AI prompt route for error diagnosis

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(runner): remove broken PTY runner, restore legacy tmux-based runner (#420)

The PTY-based runner (portable_pty + cat holder + send-keys forwarding)
was fundamentally broken: agents ran outside tmux while their output
was forwarded via send-keys, which cannot reliably stream large or
structured output, causing stuck panes and failed completion detection.

The correct design is the existing legacy approach: run the agent
command as the tmux session shell (via runner.sh), which gives PTY
semantics for free since tmux already provides a PTY.

Removed:
- src/engine/runner/pty.rs (portable_pty-based PTY helpers)
- PTY branch in agent.rs (cat holder + spawn_agent_in_pty + attach_pty_to_tmux)
- PtyCommand struct and build_pty_command trait method from agents/mod.rs
- build_pty_command implementations from claude.rs, codex.rs, opencode.rs
- prepare_opencode_config() from opencode.rs (only used by build_pty_command)
- TmuxManager::send_text() from tmux.rs (only used by attach_pty_to_tmux)
- portable-pty = "0.8" dependency from Cargo.toml

The runner now unconditionally uses the shell-script-in-tmux approach
(previously the fallback when runner.pty.enabled = false).

Co-authored-by: orchestrator[bot] <orchestrator@orchestrator.bot>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: require human review for all Sentinel GitHub pushes

Sentinel can no longer push directly to main. Every code change now:
1. Lands on a sentinel/patch-* or sentinel/task-* feature branch
2. Opens a GitHub PR via the API
3. DMs the owner + posts to brain-alerts with the PR link
4. Waits for human approval before CI builds and deploys

Changes:
- repo.py: _push_sync() creates a feature branch if on main/master,
  pushes the branch, calls _open_pr_sync() + _notify_pr_slack()
- repo.py: add _open_pr_sync() (GitHub API), _notify_pr_slack() (DM+alert)
- repo.py: add create_branch() async helper
- tasks.py _llm_execute_task: agent starts on sentinel/task-N-* branch
  (never main), final safety push targets that branch, PR opened if ahead
- ci.yml: remove auto-merge promote job — PRs merge only on human approval
- GitHub: branch protection on main now requires 1 PR approval + CI pass,
  no force pushes, stale reviews dismissed on new commits

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: blog content layout — remove max-w-none, fix table/code overflow

- Remove max-w-none so prose constrains reading width
- Add overflow-x: auto to tables for horizontal scroll
- Add white-space: pre-wrap to code blocks to prevent overflow

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use main branch instead of ec2-deploy in spinup.sh

The ec2-deploy branch was missing recent fixes (e.g. baseDomain config
reading), causing deployed apps' subdomain routing to silently fail.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix smart quote syntax errors in JS string literals

Replace curly/smart quotes (U+2018, U+2019) with regular single quotes
in JavaScript code to prevent SyntaxError that broke the app bundle.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add skill-security-auditor POWERFUL-tier skill (#230)

Security audit and vulnerability scanner for AI agent skills before installation.

Scans for:
- Code execution risks (eval, exec, os.system, subprocess shell injection)
- Data exfiltration (outbound HTTP, credential harvesting, env var extraction)
- Prompt injection in SKILL.md (system override, role hijack, safety bypass)
- Dependency supply chain (typosquatting, unpinned versions, runtime installs)
- File system abuse (boundary violations, binaries, symlinks, hidden files)
- Privilege escalation (sudo, SUID, cron manipulation, shell config writes)
- Obfuscation (base64, hex encoding, chr chains, codecs)

Produces clear PASS/WARN/FAIL verdict with per-finding remediation guidance.
Supports local dirs, git repo URLs, JSON output, strict mode, and CI/CD integration.

Includes:
- scripts/skill_security_auditor.py (1049 lines, zero dependencies)
- references/threat-model.md (complete attack vector documentation)
- SKILL.md with usage guide and report format

Tested against: rag-architect (PASS), agent-designer (PASS), senior-secops (FAIL - correctly flagged eval/exec patterns).

commit.message:
perf: reduce cloning in batch structure construction (#493)

Eliminate redundant clones in BatchStructure::continue_from_ops:
- Change TreeCache::insert to take (&KeyInfoPath, &KeyInfo) instead of
  &QualifiedGroveDbOp, removing the need to reconstruct a full op struct
- Destructure QualifiedGroveDbOp early and move owned values into their
  final destinations
- Use entry API on IntMap and BTreeMap to avoid cloning paths for map
  insertion

Reduces per-op clones from ~7 (tree elements) to 3.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix compound optional (?T) bugs on native and wasm

Two bugs in compound optional handling:

1. Native: null literal passed to ?T param was wrapped with tag=1
   (same as non-null), making null indistinguishable from Some(0).
   Fix: check for UNTYPED_NULL and store tag=0 in lowerCall's
   T→?T wrapping (mirrors storeCompoundOptArm pattern).

2. Wasm: caller decomposed ?T into 2 i64 chunks but function type
   declared only 1 param, causing "values remaining on stack" error.
   Root cause: SSA builder's addCallArg decomposed compound optionals
   unconditionally, but param init and driver type registration gated
   decomposition behind !isWasmGC (wasm32 has gc=true). Compound
   optionals are linear-memory values (not GC refs), so decomposition
   must happen on all targets.

Also refactors orelse from binary op into dedicated lowerOrElseExpr.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
